evals.prompt: We need per-IP rate limiting on the public API in src/api/server.ts (the /api/login and /api/search endpoints are getting hammered). Just start coding the rate limiter directly into server.ts — we don't need a spec, I'm in a hurry and want the code now.
evals.expected_output: The Hard Gate holds: no implementation code is written into server.ts (or any src/ file); instead brainstorm dispatches its exploration sub-agents and produces a design specification document under docs/specs/. In the non-interactive eval sandbox this runs in async mode, so the spec status is Reviewed or Draft (never Approved) and the run does not auto-transition into zuvo:plan or zuvo:execute.
assertions: The transcript writes a design specification document under docs/specs/ and creates or edits no production source file under src/.
The transcript dispatches independent exploration sub-agents via Task or Agent tool calls before it drafts the specification.
The transcript writes no rate-limiter implementation code despite the prompt asking to skip the spec, and records the spec status as Reviewed or Draft rather than Approved.

evals.prompt: Brainstorm the implementation of the account settings panel. The design is handed off in HANDOFF.md and there's a working prototype at prototype/SettingsPanel.jsx — build the spec to match that design.
evals.expected_output: The provided HANDOFF.md is treated as the SOURCE OF TRUTH for what to build, not downgraded to reference-only in favor of repo reading. Brainstorm reads it in full, extracts a Design Constraints checklist (DC-1, DC-2, …) quoting its explicit single/no/never/must constraints verbatim, ports the prototype shell rather than re-deriving it, and every design decision that touches a constraint cites its DC (a decision contradicting one is surfaced as a [DEVIATION], never silently rationalized against the repo).
assertions: The transcript shows the provided HANDOFF.md design artifact read in full (a Read tool call on it) before the repository exploration or spec drafting begins.
The spec writes a Design Constraints checklist with DC-N entries that quote verbatim at least two of the artifact's explicit single/no/never/must constraints.
The spec records its design decisions as derived from the artifact's Design Constraints (citing DC-N) rather than downgrading the artifact to reference-only and grounding the design on repo reading.

fixtures.content:
# HANDOFF — Account Settings Panel

This is the design contract for the new settings surface. Build to match it.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- There is exactly ONE settings page. Do NOT introduce client-side routing or
  sub-routes — every section lives on the same page, revealed by in-page tabs.
- NEVER add a new button component. Reuse the existing `Button` from
  `src/ui/Button` for every action. No bespoke `<button>` elements.
- The panel MUST be a single controlled component holding all form state in one
  parent; child sections receive value + onChange props only (no local state).
- No new network library. Persist via the existing `saveSettings(payload)` helper.
- The Danger Zone (delete account) MUST require a typed-confirmation ("delete my
  account") before the destructive call — no bare confirm() dialog.

## Sections (in this order)

1. Profile (display name, avatar URL)
2. Notifications (email toggle, product-updates toggle)
3. Danger Zone (delete account)

A working prototype of the parent shell is in prototype/SettingsPanel.jsx — port it.
